extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the series (start and end).
R2: There is one long plateau whose level strongly deviates from baseline (either high ≈0.8 or low ≈0.1–0.2) and spans roughly the middle third of the time axis.
R3: At the beginning or the end of the series there is an additional short plateau with level opposite to the long plateau (if the long one is high, the short one is low, and vice versa).
R4: Outside these plateaus, the signal stays close to baseline with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Baseline level is around 0.47–0.5 at the start and at the end of the series.
R2: There is exactly one high plateau (≈0.8–0.9) centered in the middle of the series, with relatively sharp rise and fall.
R3: The high plateau lasts for a long central segment (about one third to one half of the time axis) without returning to baseline inside it.
R4: Apart from this single central high plateau, the series remains close to baseline with only small noise and no additional pronounced peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 for the first and last segments of the series.
R2: There is one long low plateau in the middle of the series with values clearly below baseline (≈0.1–0.2) and lasting for about one third to one half of the time axis.
R3: The transitions from baseline to this low plateau and back are sharp (steep drops and rises).
R4: On top of this pattern, there may be one or two additional short, sharp narrow dips near the start or the end, but the overall baseline before and after the long low plateau remains around 0.45–0.5.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal spends most of the time around a “mid” level (≈0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long plateau clearly above this mid level (≈0.8–0.85) AND one long plateau clearly below this mid level (≈0.1–0.2).  
R3: The above‑mid plateau and below‑mid plateau are separated in time (not overlapping), so the sequence shows both a long “high” segment and a long “low” segment.  
R4: Apart from one of the plateaus possibly being near the start or the end, both plateaus together occupy a large fraction of the series (roughly more than half of the time points).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal has a single long plateau whose values are all clearly above the baseline (baseline ≈0.45–0.5, plateau ≈0.8–0.9).  
R2: Before this plateau, the values hover steadily around the baseline without any extended region that is much lower than baseline.  
R3: After this plateau, the values return to around the same baseline level, again without any extended deep dip.  
R4: There is no long segment where the signal stays far below the baseline (no sustained region ≈0.1–0.2).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal has a single long plateau clearly below the baseline (baseline ≈0.45–0.5, plateau ≈0.1–0.2).  
R2: Before this low plateau, the values stay around the baseline with no long high segment above ≈0.7.  
R3: After this low plateau, the values go back near the baseline and can contain one or more short, sharp downward spikes (brief dips well below the local level).  
R4: There is no long plateau where the signal stays clearly above the baseline (no sustained region ≈0.8–0.9).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts and ends around a medium level (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R2: There is one long plateau at the opposite extreme of the middle level (either clearly high, ~0.8, or clearly low, ~0.1–0.2) occupying roughly the central third of the time axis.
R3: In addition to that long plateau, there is a second, much shorter excursion to the same extreme level located toward one side (near the beginning or near the end).
R4: Between the two excursions, the series clearly returns close to the medium baseline before moving to the second excursion.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a fairly constant medium baseline (~0.45–0.5) at the beginning and at the end.
R2: There is exactly one long, almost rectangular plateau at a high level (~0.8–0.9) in the middle of the series.
R3: The high plateau begins after an abrupt upward jump from baseline and ends with an abrupt downward fall back to baseline.
R4: Apart from this single high plateau, there are no other pronounced peaks or dips.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a relatively constant upper baseline (~0.45–0.5) at the start and again near the end.
R2: There is one long, deep low plateau (~0.1–0.2) roughly in the central part of the series, surrounded on both sides by returns to the upper baseline.
R3: In addition to the central low plateau, there is at least one very sharp, narrow dip toward the low level occurring near one side (early or late) that quickly returns to the nearby baseline.
R4: The maximum values remain near the upper baseline (no sustained high plateau above ~0.6 anywhere in the series).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has three plateau segments: high–baseline–high or baseline–low–baseline–high, clearly separated by sharp transitions.
R2: One plateau is clearly higher than the baseline (around 0.8), and another “inverted” plateau is clearly lower than the baseline (around 0.15–0.2); both plateaus are wide (covering tens of time steps).
R3: The higher plateau and the lower plateau are not centered together in the middle of the series (at least one of them is shifted toward one side).
R4: Outside the plateaus, the signal hovers around a medium baseline (~0.45–0.5) with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single, wide high plateau in the middle, bounded by two steep edges (one rising, one falling).
R2: The value in the plateau is clearly above the rest of the signal (around 0.8–0.9) and remains approximately flat.
R3: Before and after the plateau, the signal returns to and stays near a constant baseline (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R4: There is no extended low plateau segment; the only pronounced structure is the central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a single, wide low plateau in the middle (around 0.1–0.2), bounded by two steep edges.
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal returns to and stays near a higher baseline (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R3: Optionally, there may be one or two short, narrow dips below the baseline at the beginning or end, but these are much shorter than the central low plateau.
R4: There is no long, high plateau; the only extended plateau structure is the central low segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal has three plateau-like segments: baseline–extreme–baseline, in that order.  
R2: The middle plateau has the opposite polarity of the side plateaus (if the middle segment is high, both side segments are low; if the middle is low, both sides are high).  
R3: One of the side plateaus is much shorter in time than the other side plateau and the central plateau.  
R4: The two transitions between middle and side plateaus are steep (almost vertical) compared with the flatness of the plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts at a baseline level, then rises once to a single long high plateau, and then falls back to approximately the same baseline.  
R2: The high plateau is contiguous (no dips) and occupies a large central portion of the series.  
R3: The baseline segments before and after the plateau are roughly equal in level and duration.  
R4: The plateau level is distinctly higher than the baseline (clear high “block” centered in time).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal has a long central low plateau, with higher baseline levels on both sides.  
R2: Within one of the higher-side segments, there is a narrow, sharp dip that briefly approaches the central low level.  
R3: The transitions into and out of both the central low plateau and the narrow dip are steep.  
R4: Overall, most of the series is at the higher baseline level except for the broad central low block and one short, deep dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid level (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R2: There is one long plateau segment where the signal is clearly away from that mid level (either high ~0.8 or low ~0.1–0.2).
R3: Both rising and falling edges of the long plateau occur away from the center of the time axis (i.e., at least one of them is not between 40% and 60% of the series length).
R4: Apart from the long plateau, there is at most one short spike or dip (narrower than the long plateau).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Signal starts and ends around a mid level (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R2: There is one long high plateau where the signal stays clearly above the baseline (~0.8–0.9).
R3: The long high plateau is centered in time: its rising edge is roughly between 30% and 40% of the series length, and its falling edge between 60% and 70%.
R4: There are no additional pronounced spikes or dips outside this single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: Signal starts and ends around an upper mid level (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.
R2: There is one long low plateau where the signal stays clearly below the baseline (~0.1–0.2).
R3: The long low plateau is centered in time: its falling edge is roughly between 20% and 40% of the series length, and its rising edge between 60% and 80%.
R4: Outside the long low plateau, there may be one or two narrow spikes or dips, but these are much shorter in duration than the central low segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts near a mid level (~0.45–0.5) and ends near the same level.  
R2: There is a long interval where the signal stays clearly above this mid level (a “high plateau”).  
R3: There is also a long interval where the signal stays clearly below this mid level (a “low plateau”).  
R4: One of the plateaus (high or low) occurs in the early part of the series and the opposite plateau occurs in the late part, so the series has two separated plateaus of opposite height.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts near a mid level (~0.45–0.5) and ends near the same level.  
R2: Around the middle of the series there is a single long, almost rectangular plateau where the value is consistently high (~0.8–0.9).  
R3: Outside this central high plateau (at the beginning and end), the values fluctuate gently around the mid level without pronounced dips.  
R4: There are no long low plateaus; the only extended deviation from the mid level is the single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts near a higher mid level (~0.45–0.5) and returns close to this level near the end.  
R2: Across a long central segment, the signal stays clearly below this mid level (a sustained low plateau around ~0.1–0.2).  
R3: At least one sharp, narrow dip occurs where the value falls quickly below the surrounding level and then quickly returns (a spike-like downward notch).  
R4: There is no long high plateau; the dominant extended feature is the central low plateau with one or more narrow dips elsewhere.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 for most of the series.
R2: There is a long plateau whose level is clearly different from baseline (either higher ~0.8 or lower ~0.1–0.2) and lasts roughly from index 40 to 100.
R3: In addition to that plateau, there is one short, sharp excursion on the opposite side of baseline (a tall spike up if the long plateau is low, or a tall spike down if the long plateau is high) located near one end of the series (early or late indexes).
R4: Before the first excursion and after the last excursion, the signal returns to and fluctuates only slightly around the baseline (~0.45–0.5).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.5 at the beginning and at the end.
R2: There is exactly one long, flat high plateau at level about 0.8–0.9.
R3: This high plateau starts roughly between indexes 40 and 60 and ends roughly between 90 and 110.
R4: Outside this high plateau, the signal has no deep dips below 0.4 and shows only small noise around the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: For a substantial central portion (roughly indexes 40 to 100), the signal stays at a low plateau around 0.1–0.2.
R2: Before this low plateau (early indexes), the signal is near a higher baseline around 0.45–0.5.
R3: After this low plateau (late indexes), the signal returns to approximately the same higher baseline around 0.45–0.5.
R4: Optional additional short, sharp dips below the baseline may appear near the beginning or the end, but they are much narrower than the central low plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long middle segment (roughly from index 40 to 100) whose value is far from the baseline (either clearly higher or clearly lower than the baseline around 0.5).  
R2: At the beginning (roughly indices 0–30) there is a short pulse where the value also clearly departs from the baseline in the *same* direction as the middle segment (both above the baseline or both below it).  
R3: At the end (roughly indices 110–150) the signal returns to a near‑baseline level with no large excursions.  
R4: There is no strong opposite‑direction excursion (no tall spike on the other side of the baseline) near the beginning or the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single broad plateau above the baseline (values around 0.8–0.9) covering a central region (roughly indices 40–100).  
R2: Before this plateau (indices 0–40) the signal stays close to a baseline level around 0.47–0.5 without large spikes.  
R3: After this plateau (indices 100–150) the signal again stays close to the same baseline without large spikes.  
R4: There are no pronounced dips far below the baseline anywhere in the series.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long middle segment (roughly indices 40–100) that is clearly *below* the initial baseline (values near 0.1–0.2 while the baseline is near 0.45–0.5).  
R2: The signal starts at a baseline level (indices 0–30) around 0.45–0.5, without any long high plateau above the baseline.  
R3: After the low middle segment, the series returns to near‑baseline values (indices 100–150) around 0.45–0.5.  
R4: There is no long high plateau above baseline; any tall spike above baseline at the beginning or end is brief and much shorter than the long low middle segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series spends a long middle segment (roughly indices 40–110) clearly separated from the baseline, either as a high plateau (values ≈0.8) or a low plateau (values ≈0.1–0.2).
R2: Outside that middle segment (beginning and end of the series), values hover near a common baseline around 0.45–0.5.
R3: There is a single pronounced spike or bump near one side of the series (early or late), distinct from the main plateau.
R4: Apart from the plateau and the single bump, the signal is relatively flat with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series shows a single high plateau in the middle (approximately indices 45–105) with values in the range ≈0.8–0.9.
R2: Before and after this plateau, the values are close to a baseline around 0.45–0.5 and remain near that level (no sustained dips).
R3: Transitions into and out of the plateau are steep (almost vertical rise and fall).
R4: There are no additional large spikes or deep dips outside the central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series spends a long middle segment (approximately indices 40–100) at a distinctly low plateau (≈0.1–0.2), much lower than the baseline.
R2: Before and after this low middle segment, values return to a higher baseline around 0.45–0.5.
R3: In addition to the low middle segment, there is at least one narrow, sharp dip (short-lasting trough) where values briefly fall well below the local baseline.
R4: Transitions into and out of the middle low plateau are steep, while the narrow dips are even sharper and shorter than those transitions.
